natural_language: I advocate for the approval of this request as it stands, with the non-negotiable red lines respected. This will unlock significant productivity gains for our employees.

claims.text: The Data Processing Agreement (DPA) with the vendor is still pending and must be signed before approval.

natural_language: I am blocking this request until the DPA with the vendor is signed. The DPA is a non-negotiable requirement for vendor risk management.

claims.text: Vendor DPA is a non-negotiable requirement for ensuring compliance with GDPR and must be signed before proceeding.

natural_language: I support the stance of Procurement & Vendor Risk. The DPA with the vendor must be signed before we can proceed, as it is a non-negotiable requirement for GDPR compliance.
I have no objections in my lane this round, as the Betriebsvereinbarung status is signed and the use case does not trigger any additional works council requirements.

claims.text: Routing sensitive traffic to LOCAL_QWEN_72B is more cost-effective and ensures data sovereignty.

natural_language: I support the stance of Procurement & Vendor Risk. The DPA must be signed before we can proceed. Additionally, I demand that sensitive traffic be routed to LOCAL_QWEN_72B for cost and data sovereignty reasons.

claims.text: While the DPA is pending, we can still move forward with a pilot to unlock productivity gains.
Routing sensitive traffic to LOCAL_QWEN_72B is a reasonable alternative to address data sovereignty and cost concerns.

natural_language: I agree with IT & Infra's suggestion to route sensitive traffic to LOCAL_QWEN_72B. This will allow us to proceed with a pilot while the DPA is being finalized, unlocking productivity for our employees.
